message:
✨ Add Diagnose and Repair action buttons to failed items in dashboard cards (#639)

Add per-item Diagnose and Repair action buttons that appear on failed/unhealthy
resources across all dashboard card types. Diagnose opens events/logs views for
the specific resource, Repair offers contextual fix options (restart, redeploy,
scale up, view YAML, etc.).

Cards updated:
- DeploymentStatus: buttons on failed deployments
- PodIssues: buttons on all pod issues
- DeploymentIssues: buttons on all deployment issues
- ClusterHealth: buttons on unhealthy/offline clusters
- SecurityIssues: buttons on all security violations
- AppStatus: buttons on apps with warnings/pending status

New reusable CardActionButtons component added to CardComponents.tsx with
portal-based repair dropdown menu.

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

left: number } | null>(null) + + useEffect(() => { + if (showRepairMenu && repairBtnRef.current) { + const rect = repairBtnRef.current.getBoundingClientRect() + setMenuPos({ + top: rect.bottom + 4, + left: Math.max(8, rect.right - REPAIR_MENU_WIDTH), + }) + } else { + setMenuPos(null) + } + }, [showRepairMenu]) + + // Close menu on outside click + useEffect(() => { + if (!showRepairMenu) return + const handleClick = () => setShowRepairMenu(false) + document.addEventListener('click', handleClick) + return () => document.removeEventListener('click', handleClick) + }, [showRepairMenu]) + + return ( +
e.stopPropagation()} + > + + +
